description: Build a native Android (Kotlin + Jetpack Compose) clone of the Wellvo iOS app with full feature parity. The Android app connects to the same Supabase backend and edge functions, uses FCM instead of APNs, Google Play Billing instead of StoreKit 2, and Room instead of SwiftData. Target Android 8.0+ (API 26+), Material Design 3.

userStories.title: Scaffold Android project with Kotlin and Jetpack Compose
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a properly structured Android project so that all subsequent features have a solid foundation to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: New android/ directory at project root with standard Gradle project structure
App module: android/app/ with build.gradle.kts using Kotlin DSL
compileSdk = 34, minSdk = 26, targetSdk = 34
Kotlin 1.9+, Jetpack Compose BOM (latest stable)
Package name: net.wellvo.android
Material Design 3 theme with Wellvo brand colors (green primary #22C55E, white background)
App name 'Wellvo' in strings.xml
Project builds successfully with ./gradlew assembleDebug
Root settings.gradle.kts includes the app module
userStories.notes: Foundation for all Android work. Use single-module architecture initially; can modularize later. Create under android/ at the repo root alongside ios/.

userStories.title: Set up dependency injection with Hilt
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Hilt DI so that services, repositories, and ViewModels are properly scoped and testable.
acceptanceCriteria: Hilt Gradle plugin and dependencies added to build.gradle.kts
@HiltAndroidApp annotation on Application class (WellvoApplication)
AppModule providing singletons: SupabaseClient, SharedPreferences, Room database
NetworkModule providing Ktor HttpClient (or OkHttp) configured with Supabase base URL
All service classes annotated with @Inject and @Singleton where appropriate
Project builds with Hilt kapt/ksp processing
userStories.notes: Hilt is the standard Android DI framework. Use KSP over KAPT for faster builds.

userStories.title: Integrate Supabase Kotlin SDK
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a configured Supabase client so that the Android app can authenticate users and query the database.
acceptanceCriteria: io.github.jan-tennert.supabase Kotlin SDK added (Auth, PostgREST, Realtime, Functions modules)
Supabase URL and anon key loaded from BuildConfig fields (set in build.gradle.kts)
Separate debug/release build flavors with different Supabase credentials if needed
SupabaseClient singleton created via Hilt with GoTrue, Postgrest, Realtime, Functions plugins
Helper extension functions for common queries (e.g., supabase.from(table).select())
Connection verified: app can reach Supabase and return a response
userStories.notes: Use the official Supabase Kotlin Multiplatform SDK (io.github.jan-tennert.supabase). Supabase URL and key should NOT be hardcoded — use BuildConfig.

userStories.title: Set up Jetpack Navigation with role-based routing
userStories.description: As a user, I want to be routed to the correct screen based on my auth state and role so that I see the right experience immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Jetpack Navigation Compose dependency added
NavHost in MainActivity with routes: splash, auth, onboarding, ownerTabs, receiverHome, viewerTabs, pairingCode, receiverOnboarding
Navigation graph mirrors iOS ContentView routing: authState (loading/unauthenticated/authenticated) → role (owner/receiver/viewer)
Owner gets bottom nav with 4 tabs: Dashboard, History, Family, Settings
Receiver gets single-screen check-in interface
Viewer gets bottom nav with 3 tabs: Dashboard (read-only), History (read-only), Settings
Back stack handled correctly (no back from authenticated to auth screen)
Splash screen shows while checking auth state
userStories.notes: Match the iOS ContentView routing logic exactly. Use sealed class for routes.

userStories.title: Configure build variants and signing
userStories.description: As a developer, I want debug and release build variants configured so that development and production builds are properly separated.
acceptanceCriteria: Debug build variant with debug Supabase credentials in BuildConfig
Release build variant with production Supabase credentials
ProGuard/R8 rules for release builds (minifyEnabled = true)
Keep rules for Supabase SDK, Ktor, Hilt, and serialization classes
Signing config placeholder for release keystore (keystore path, alias, passwords from local.properties or environment variables)
Version code and version name set in build.gradle.kts
./gradlew assembleDebug and ./gradlew assembleRelease both succeed
userStories.notes: Do NOT commit keystore or passwords. Use local.properties or CI environment variables.

userStories.title: Implement core data models with Kotlin Serialization
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Kotlin data classes matching the Supabase schema so that API responses deserialize correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Kotlinx Serialization dependency added with JSON format
AppUser data class: id (UUID), email, phone, displayName, role (enum: owner/receiver/viewer), avatarUrl, timezone, createdAt, updatedAt, lastSeenAt, lastBatteryLevel, lastAppVersion

Family data class: id, name, ownerId, subscriptionTier (enum: free/family/family_plus), subscriptionStatus (enum: active/expired/grace_period/cancelled), subscriptionExpiresAt, maxReceivers, maxViewers, createdAt
FamilyMember data class: id, familyId, userId, role (enum: owner/receiver/viewer), status (enum: active/invited/deactivated), invitedAt, joinedAt, user (nullable joined field)

CheckIn data class: id, receiverId, familyId, checkedInAt, mood (enum), source (enum: app/notification/on_demand/need_help/call_me), scheduledFor, responseType, latitude, longitude, locationAccuracyMeters, distanceFromHomeMeters, locationLabel, kidResponseType

CheckInRequest data class: id, familyId, receiverId, requestedBy, type (enum: scheduled/on_demand), status (enum: pending/checked_in/missed/expired), createdAt, respondedAt, escalationStep, nextEscalationAt
All @Serializable with @SerialName for snake_case mapping
Nullable fields properly annotated
userStories.notes: Match iOS models exactly. Use kotlinx.serialization @SerialName for snake_case column names. All timestamp fields as String or Instant.

userStories.title: Implement ReceiverSettings and schedule models
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ReceiverSettings data classes so that per-receiver configuration can be loaded and saved.
acceptanceCriteria: ReceiverSettings data class with all fields: id, familyMemberId, checkinTime (HH:mm string), timezone, gracePeriodMinutes, reminderIntervalMinutes, escalationEnabled, quietHoursStart, quietHoursEnd, moodTrackingEnabled, smsEscalationEnabled, isActive, locationTrackingEnabled, homeLatitude, homeLongitude, geofenceRadiusMeters, locationAlertEnabled, receiverMode (enum: standard/kid), scheduleType (enum: daily/weekday_weekend/custom), weekendCheckinTime, customSchedule (list of DaySchedule), schedulePaused
DaySchedule data class: dayOfWeek (Int 1-7), isEnabled (Boolean), checkinTime (HH:mm string)
ReceiverMode enum: standard (3 moods), kid (8 moods)
ScheduleType enum: daily, weekday_weekend, custom
Mood enum with displayName, emoji, and color for each: happy, neutral, tired (standard) + excited, bored, hungry, scared, having_fun (kid)
LocationLabel enum: home, school, friends_house, park, store, other with display names
KidResponseType enum: picking_me_up, can_stay_longer, sos with labels, icons, and colors
WellvoAlert data class: id, familyId, receiverId, type, title, message, data (Map), isRead, createdAt
userStories.notes: These enums drive UI rendering. Match iOS emoji/color choices exactly.

userStories.title: Set up Room database for offline storage
userStories.description: As a receiver, I want my check-ins saved locally when offline so that they sync when I'm back online.
acceptanceCriteria: Room dependency added (room-runtime, room-ktx, room-compiler with KSP)
OfflineCheckIn @Entity with fields: id (UUID primary key), familyId, receiverId, mood (String nullable), source (String), createdAt (Long timestamp), synced (Boolean default false)
OfflineCheckInDao with: insert, getUnsynced (WHERE synced = false), markSynced (by id), deleteAll
WellvoDatabase @Database class with OfflineCheckIn entity
Database singleton provided via Hilt AppModule
Migration strategy: fallbackToDestructiveMigration for v1 (no production data yet)
userStories.notes: Equivalent of iOS SwiftData OfflineCheckIn model. Room is the standard Android local DB. Implemented in US-A002.

userStories.title: Implement network layer and API service
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a clean network layer so that edge function calls and Supabase queries are consistent and reliable.
acceptanceCriteria: ApiService class with methods matching all edge function endpoints: processCheckinResponse, onDemandCheckin, confirmDelivery, heartbeat, reportLocation, inviteReceiver, redeemCode, autoJoin, subscriptionWebhook
Each method calls the edge function via Supabase Functions invoke or direct HTTP
Request/response DTOs as @Serializable data classes
Consistent error handling: network errors → WellvoError.Network, auth errors → WellvoError.Auth, 404 → WellvoError.NotFound, 5xx → WellvoError.ServerError
WellvoError sealed class with: Network, Auth, NotFound, ServerError, Offline, Unknown variants, each with localizedMessage
NetworkRetry utility: suspend function with exponential backoff, max 3 attempts, skips auth/cancellation errors
Timeout of 30 seconds on all requests
userStories.notes: Mirrors iOS NetworkRetry and WellvoError. Use Ktor client from Supabase SDK or standalone OkHttp.

userStories.title: Implement secure configuration and credential storage
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Supabase credentials loaded from BuildConfig and sensitive data stored in Android Keystore so that secrets are not hardcoded.
acceptanceCriteria: SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY injected via buildConfigField in build.gradle.kts
Values read from local.properties (not committed) with fallback to environment variables
EncryptedSharedPreferences (AndroidX Security) for storing auth tokens and sensitive user data
KeychainService equivalent: SecureStorage class wrapping EncryptedSharedPreferences with save/load/delete methods
Google user ID stored securely (equivalent to iOS Keychain Apple User ID storage)
No secrets in version control (local.properties in .gitignore)
userStories.notes: Equivalent of iOS BuildConfig.xcconfig + KeychainService. EncryptedSharedPreferences uses Android Keystore under the hood.

userStories.title: Implement phone OTP authentication
userStories.description: As a receiver, I want to sign in with my phone number via SMS code so that I can join my family's Wellvo group without creating a password.
acceptanceCriteria: AuthService class with sendPhoneOTP(phone: String) and verifyPhoneOTP(phone: String, code: String) methods
Phone number normalization: strip non-digits, prepend +1 for 10-digit US numbers
Supabase Auth signInWithOtp used for phone flow
AuthViewModel state: phoneNumber, otpCode, isAwaitingOTP, isLoading, errorMessage
OTP entry UI: phone input with country code → 6-digit code input with auto-focus
Error states: invalid phone format, wrong OTP code, expired OTP, rate limited
After successful OTP: check session, load user profile, determine role, navigate appropriately
userStories.notes: Phone OTP is the PRIMARY auth method for receivers. Must match iOS flow exactly. Supabase handles the SMS sending.

userStories.title: Implement email/password authentication
userStories.description: As an owner, I want to sign up and sign in with email and password so that I have a secure account for managing my family.
acceptanceCriteria: AuthService signUpWithEmail(email, password, displayName) and signInWithEmail(email, password) methods
Password validation: 8+ characters, at least one uppercase, one lowercase, one number
Email validation: basic format check
Display name validation: 2-50 characters
Sign-up creates user profile in users table after auth
Sign-in fetches existing profile
AuthViewModel state: email, password, displayName (for sign-up), authMode (signIn/signUp) toggle
UI: tabbed sign-in / sign-up forms with validation error display
AuthError sealed class: InvalidEmail, PasswordTooShort, PasswordTooWeak, InvalidDisplayName, UserNotFound, InvalidCredential
userStories.notes: Email/password is primarily for Owners. Match iOS validation rules exactly.

userStories.title: Implement Google Sign-In
userStories.description: As a user, I want to sign in with my Google account so that I can use one-tap authentication without remembering a password.
acceptanceCriteria: Google Sign-In dependency added (Credential Manager API or legacy Google Sign-In)
AuthService signInWithGoogle() method using Supabase Auth signInWith(Google) provider
Google OAuth client ID configured in build.gradle.kts (from Google Cloud Console)
One-tap sign-in prompt on auth screen
ID token sent to Supabase for server-side verification
User profile created/updated after successful Google sign-in
AuthViewModel tracks Google sign-in state and errors
Fallback to standard Google Sign-In flow if Credential Manager unavailable
userStories.notes: Android equivalent of Apple Sign-In. Uses Credential Manager with GetGoogleIdOption. ID token sent to Supabase via IDToken provider with Google. GOOGLE_WEB_CLIENT_ID from BuildConfig (local.properties or env var). ProGuard keep rules added for Credential Manager and googleid library.

userStories.title: Implement auth state management and session persistence
userStories.description: As a user, I want to stay signed in across app restarts so that I don't have to re-authenticate every time.
acceptanceCriteria: AuthViewModel observes Supabase auth state changes (sessionStatus flow)
AuthState sealed class: Loading, Unauthenticated, Authenticated(user: AppUser)
On app start: check for existing session, refresh if expired, route accordingly
Session token persisted by Supabase SDK (SettingsSessionManager or custom)
Sign-out clears session, tokens, cached data, and navigates to auth screen
Auth state exposed as StateFlow for Compose observation
Handle token refresh failures gracefully (re-authenticate prompt)
userStories.notes: Equivalent of iOS AuthViewModel.listenForAuthStateChanges(). Supabase Kotlin SDK handles token persistence.

userStories.title: Implement auto-join and pairing code flows
userStories.description: As a receiver who was invited, I want to automatically join my family after phone OTP so that setup is seamless.
acceptanceCriteria: AuthService checkAutoJoin() method: calls auto-join edge function with user's phone number
If match found: auto-accepts invite, returns family info, routes to receiver onboarding
If no match: proceeds to normal onboarding
PairingCodeViewModel with state: code (6 digits), isLoading, errorMessage, success (family name + check-in time)
PairingCodeScreen: 6-digit input field, submit button, success state with check-in time display
redeemPairingCode() calls redeem-code edge function
On success: routes to receiver home
On failure: shows error (invalid code, expired, already used)
userStories.notes: Auto-join matches the receiver's phone number to pending invitations. Pairing code is for iPad/alternate device setup on iOS — same flow on Android.

userStories.title: Build authentication UI screens
userStories.description: As a user, I want a polished authentication screen so that I can sign in or create an account easily.
acceptanceCriteria: AuthScreen composable with Wellvo logo, app name, and tagline at top
Three auth options: Phone OTP (primary, prominent), Google Sign-In button, Email/Password (expandable section)
Phone flow: phone number input with +1 prefix → OTP code input → loading → success
Email flow: sign-in/sign-up tab toggle, form fields with validation, submit button
Google Sign-In: branded Google button following Google's design guidelines
Error messages displayed inline below relevant fields
Loading states with CircularProgressIndicator
Keyboard handling: IME actions (next/done), auto-focus, dismiss on submit
Material Design 3 styling consistent with Wellvo brand
userStories.notes: Match iOS AuthView layout and flow. Phone OTP should be the most prominent option.

userStories.title: Implement owner onboarding flow (7 steps)
userStories.description: As a new owner, I want a guided setup flow so that I can create my family group, invite my first receiver, and understand how Wellvo works.
acceptanceCriteria: OnboardingViewModel with currentStep (enum: welcome, userType, createFamily, choosePlan, addReceiver, notifications, complete)
Step 1 - Welcome: app intro with illustration, 'Get Started' button
Step 2 - User Type: selection cards for aging_parent, teenager, other with descriptions
Step 3 - Create Family: family name input, create button, calls FamilyService.createFamily()
Step 4 - Choose Plan: three plan cards (Free/Family/Family+) with features and prices
Step 5 - Add Receiver: name input, phone input, check-in time picker, calls FamilyService.inviteReceiver()
Step 6 - Notifications: permission request explanation, request button, calls notification permission
Step 7 - Complete: success message with next steps, 'Go to Dashboard' button
Back navigation between steps, progress indicator at top
State preserved across configuration changes (SavedStateHandle)
userStories.notes: Exact mirror of iOS OnboardingView. 7-step wizard with back/forward navigation.

userStories.title: Implement receiver onboarding flow (3 steps)
userStories.description: As a new receiver, I want a quick setup so that I understand when my check-in is scheduled and how notifications work.
acceptanceCriteria: ReceiverOnboardingScreen composable with 3 steps
Step 1 - Welcome: greeting with receiver's name, explanation of daily check-in, display scheduled check-in time
Step 2 - Notifications: explain why notifications matter, request notification permission button
Step 3 - Complete: 'You're all set!' message, 'Start Using Wellvo' button → navigate to ReceiverHome
Check-in time loaded from receiver_settings for this family member
Progress dots at bottom
Handles notification permission denied gracefully (warns but allows proceeding)
userStories.notes: Simpler than owner onboarding. 3 steps: welcome → notifications → done.

userStories.title: Implement FamilyService for family management
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a FamilyService so that family CRUD, member management, and invitation logic is centralized.
acceptanceCriteria: FamilyService @Singleton with @Inject constructor
createFamily(name: String): creates family in Supabase, returns Family
getFamily(userId: String): fetches family where user is a member
getFamilyMembers(familyId: String): returns list of FamilyMember with joined user data
getCurrentUserRole(userId: String, familyId: String): returns Role enum
inviteReceiver(familyId, name, phone, checkinTime): calls invite-receiver edge function
removeMember(memberId: String): deactivates family member
acceptInvite(token: String): calls invite-receiver edge function with action='accept'
redeemPairingCode(code: String): calls redeem-code edge function
checkAutoJoin(phone: String): calls auto-join edge function
All methods throw WellvoError on failure
userStories.notes: Direct port of iOS FamilyService. All edge function calls use the same endpoints.

userStories.title: Build receiver check-in screen (standard mode)
userStories.description: As a receiver, I want a simple one-button interface so that I can check in with a single tap.
acceptanceCriteria: ReceiverHomeScreen composable with large centered 'I'm OK' button (green, circular, prominent)
ReceiverViewModel with state: hasCheckedInToday, isCheckingIn, lastCheckIn, errorMessage, nextCheckInTime
Before check-in: large green button with 'I'm OK' text
During check-in: loading indicator on button
After check-in: green checkmark with 'You're all set!' status card, time of check-in displayed
Next check-in time displayed at bottom
Haptic feedback on successful check-in (HapticFeedbackType.Confirm)
Error state with retry option
Loads today's check-in status on screen entry
userStories.notes: This is THE core receiver experience. Must be dead simple. Match iOS ReceiverHomeView exactly.

userStories.title: Implement CheckInService
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a CheckInService so that check-in logic including location, battery, and mood is centralized.
acceptanceCriteria: CheckInService @Singleton with @Inject constructor
checkIn(familyId, receiverId, mood?, location?, batteryLevel?): calls process-checkin-response edge function
respondToCheckIn(requestId, source, responseType, location?, batteryLevel?): responds to notification-triggered check-in
confirmDelivery(checkinRequestId): calls confirm-delivery edge function
sendOnDemandCheckIn(familyId, receiverId): calls on-demand-checkin edge function
todayCheckInStatus(receiverId, familyId): queries checkins table for today's record
checkInHistory(receiverId, familyId, days: Int): queries checkins for date range
Battery level obtained from BatteryManager
Location obtained from FusedLocationProviderClient
userStories.notes: Direct port of iOS CheckInService. Same edge function endpoints.

userStories.title: Implement mood selection for check-ins
userStories.description: As a receiver, I want to optionally share my mood after checking in so that my family knows how I'm feeling.
acceptanceCriteria: MoodSelectorComposable: grid of mood options that appears after check-in
Standard mode (3 moods): Happy 😊, Neutral 😐, Tired 😴 — displayed as horizontal row
Kid mode (8 moods): Happy 😊, Neutral 😐, Tired 😴, Excited 🤩, Bored 😑, Hungry 🍕, Scared 😨, Having Fun 🎉 — displayed as 2×4 grid
Each mood is a tappable card with emoji and label
Selected mood highlighted with border/background color
Mood submitted via CheckInService update (PATCH to checkins table)
Mood selection is optional — can be skipped
ReceiverViewModel.selectedMood state, submitMood() method
userStories.notes: Match iOS mood emojis and grid layout exactly. Standard vs kid mode determined by receiverSettings.receiverMode.

userStories.title: Implement kid mode features (location label and response buttons)
userStories.description: As a kid-mode receiver, I want to share where I am and send quick requests so that my parent knows my situation.
acceptanceCriteria: LocationLabelSelector composable: 3×2 grid of location options (Home 🏠, School 🏫, Friend's House 🏡, Park 🌳, Store 🏪, Other 📍)
Each location is a tappable card with emoji and label
Selected location submitted via CheckInService update (PATCH locationLabel on checkins)
KidResponseButtons composable: horizontal row of 3 action buttons
Pick Me Up 🚗 (orange), Can I Stay Longer? ⏰ (blue), SOS! ⚠️ (red)
Kid response submitted via CheckInService update (PATCH kidResponseType on checkins)
Both selectors appear after mood selection in kid mode
ReceiverViewModel: selectedLocationLabel, selectedKidResponse, submitLocationLabel(), submitKidResponse()
All kid mode UI only visible when receiverSettings.receiverMode == .kid
userStories.notes: Kid mode adds location and response features after the basic check-in. Match iOS ReceiverHomeView kid mode section.

userStories.title: Implement offline check-in with Room and sync
userStories.description: As a receiver without internet, I want my check-in saved locally and synced automatically when connectivity returns.
acceptanceCriteria: OfflineCheckInService @Singleton with connectivity monitoring
ConnectivityManager.NetworkCallback used to monitor network state
isOffline StateFlow<Boolean> exposed for UI
performCheckIn(): tries online first, queues to Room if network fails
queueCheckIn(): inserts OfflineCheckIn entity with synced=false
syncPendingCheckIns(): fetches unsynced, calls CheckInService for each, marks synced on success
Sync triggered automatically when network becomes available
pendingOfflineCount StateFlow<Int> for UI badge
ReceiverHomeScreen shows offline indicator banner when isOffline
ReceiverHomeScreen shows 'X check-ins pending sync' when pendingOfflineCount > 0
userStories.notes: Direct port of iOS OfflineCheckInService. Uses Room instead of SwiftData, ConnectivityManager instead of NWPathMonitor.

userStories.title: Implement receiver settings loading and next check-in computation
userStories.description: As a receiver, I want to see when my next check-in is scheduled so that I know when to expect notifications.
acceptanceCriteria: ReceiverViewModel.loadReceiverSettings(): fetches ReceiverSettings from Supabase for current family member
computeNextCheckInTime(): calculates next check-in based on scheduleType
Daily: returns checkinTime for today (or tomorrow if past)
Weekday/Weekend: returns weekday or weekend time based on current day
Custom: finds next enabled day's time from customSchedule list
Respects timezone from receiver settings
Handles schedulePaused state (shows 'Notifications paused' instead of time)
nextCheckInTime displayed on ReceiverHomeScreen below check-in button
userStories.notes: Match iOS ReceiverViewModel.computeNextCheckInTime() logic for all 3 schedule types.

userStories.title: Build owner dashboard screen
userStories.description: As an owner, I want a dashboard showing all my receivers' check-in status so that I can see at a glance if everyone is OK.
acceptanceCriteria: DashboardScreen composable with pull-to-refresh
DashboardViewModel with state: family, receiverCards (list), weeklySummary, alerts, isLoading, errorMessage
Notification permission banner at top if notifications not granted
Alerts section: pattern alerts (e.g., time drift) with dismiss button
Weekly summary card: consistency %, average check-in time, total check-ins (X/Y), mood breakdown with emojis
Receiver status cards (one per active receiver) with: name, status (checked_in/pending/missed/no_data), streak count, last check-in time, mood emoji, 'Check on' button for pending receivers
Status colors: green (checked in), yellow (pending), red (missed), gray (no data)
Streak calculation: consecutive days with on-time check-ins
loadDashboard() fetches family, members, today's check-ins, alerts
userStories.notes: Core owner screen. Match iOS DashboardView layout. ReceiverStatusCard data class mirrors iOS.

userStories.title: Implement real-time dashboard updates
userStories.description: As an owner, I want the dashboard to update in real-time when a receiver checks in so that I see status changes immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: DashboardViewModel subscribes to Supabase Realtime on checkins table filtered by familyId
On INSERT event: reload the affected receiver's card, update weekly summary
Realtime channel created on dashboard entry, unsubscribed on exit
Uses Supabase Kotlin SDK realtime postgresChangeFlow()
Handles reconnection gracefully (re-subscribes on connection restore)
Full dashboard refresh available via pull-to-refresh as fallback
No UI jank: updates applied on main dispatcher
userStories.notes: Uses Supabase Realtime Postgres Changes. Same approach as iOS but with Kotlin coroutine flows.

userStories.title: Implement today's timeline on dashboard
userStories.description: As an owner, I want a visual timeline showing each receiver's check-in progress for today.
acceptanceCriteria: ReceiverTimelineComposable: 24-hour horizontal bar per receiver
Colored dot at left: green (checked in), yellow (pending), red (missed), gray (no data)
If checked in: show time label on the bar at the check-in position
If pending/missed: show status text
Receivers ordered by check-in time (checked in first, then pending, then missed)
Timeline integrated into DashboardScreen below weekly summary
Accessible: each timeline item has contentDescription with receiver name and status
userStories.notes: Implemented ReceiverTimelineComposable with 24-hour horizontal bar, status-based sorting, time label on bar, colored status dot, and accessibility contentDescription.

userStories.title: Implement kid mode indicators on dashboard cards
userStories.description: As an owner, I want to see kid-mode receiver details (location, response, extended moods) on their dashboard card.
acceptanceCriteria: ReceiverStatusCard includes optional locationLabel, kidResponseType fields
If receiver is in kid mode and checked in: show location label emoji + text below mood
If kid response submitted: show response badge (Pick Me Up/Stay Longer/SOS) with color
SOS badge highlighted in red with exclamation icon
'Pick Me Up' shown in orange, 'Stay Longer' in blue
Kid mode receivers show extended mood emoji set
Notification warning icon if receiver's push token not registered
userStories.notes: All kid mode indicators already implemented in DashboardScreen. Fixed KidResponseType colors: PickUp changed to orange (0xFFF97316), StayLonger changed to blue (0xFF3B82F6) per acceptance criteria. SOS remains red with warning icon. Location label, extended moods, and notification warning all present.

userStories.title: Implement on-demand check-in from dashboard
userStories.description: As an owner, I want to send an immediate check-in request to a receiver so that I can check on them outside the schedule.
acceptanceCriteria: Each pending/missed receiver card has 'Check on [name]' button
Button calls DashboardViewModel.sendOnDemandCheckIn(receiverId)
DashboardViewModel calls CheckInService.sendOnDemandCheckIn(familyId, receiverId)
Success: show snackbar 'Check-in request sent to [name]'
Loading state on button while sending
Error: show snackbar with error message
Button disabled for receivers who already checked in today
Rate limiting: disable button for 60 seconds after sending (prevent spam)
userStories.notes: Implemented on-demand check-in with: loading spinner on button while sending, success snackbar, error snackbar, 60-second cooldown timer with countdown display, button disabled for checked-in receivers and during cooldown.

userStories.title: Build family management screen
userStories.description: As an owner, I want to see and manage all family members so that I can add, remove, and configure receivers.
acceptanceCriteria: FamilyScreen composable with family name and subscription tier badge at top
Member list: each member shows name, role badge, status (active/invited/deactivated)
Swipe-to-dismiss actions: Remove member (with confirmation dialog)
Long-press context menu: Transfer ownership, Re-send invite (for invited members)
FAB or top-bar button: 'Invite Receiver' → opens InviteReceiverSheet
FamilyViewModel with state: family, members, isLoading, errorMessage
loadFamily() fetches family + members via FamilyService
removeMember() with confirmation dialog → calls FamilyService.removeMember()
Empty state: 'No receivers yet. Invite someone to get started.'
userStories.notes: Verified: FamilyScreen.kt, FamilyViewModel.kt, FamilyService.kt all complete. Integrated in OwnerTabsScreen as tab 2. All 9 acceptance criteria satisfied.

userStories.title: Implement invite receiver flow
userStories.description: As an owner, I want to invite a receiver by phone number so that they receive an SMS with setup instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: InviteReceiverSheet composable (bottom sheet or dialog): name input, phone input, check-in time picker
Phone validation: accepts US formats ((xxx) xxx-xxxx, xxx-xxx-xxxx, +1xxxxxxxxxx, xxxxxxxxxx)
Inline error for invalid phone format, send button disabled until valid
Time picker: Material 3 TimePicker for selecting daily check-in time
Submit calls FamilyService.inviteReceiver(familyId, name, phone, checkinTime)
Success state: checkmark icon, 'Invitation sent!' message, 'How to set up' instructions
Instructions include: receiver will get SMS, download app, enter code or sign in with phone
Error handling: member limit reached, invalid phone, network error
Sheet dismissable after success
userStories.notes: Enhanced InviteReceiverSheet: M3 TimePicker, ViewModel-driven async invite with error handling, phone validation with inline errors, success state with checkmark and setup instructions.

userStories.title: Implement receiver setup guide view
userStories.description: As an owner who just invited a receiver, I want setup guidance so that I can help my receiver get started.
acceptanceCriteria: ReceiverSetupGuideScreen composable accessible from invite success state

userStories.notes: Verified: HistoryScreen.kt, HistoryViewModel.kt fully implemented. Wired into OwnerTabsScreen as tab 1. All 8 acceptance criteria satisfied.

userStories.title: Implement alert display and dismissal

userStories.notes: Implemented. WeeklySummary data class with consistency%, avgTime, totalActual/Expected, mood breakdown. computeWeeklySummary() calculates from 7-day check-ins. WeeklySummaryCard composable with color-coded consistency (green/yellow/red), avg time, total check-ins X/Y, mood emoji counts. Empty state handled with 0%.

PushNotificationService singleton with registerToken, checkPermissionStatus, requiresPermissionRequest. POST_NOTIFICATIONS permission in manifest.

confirmDelivery() called when notification received
userStories.notes: Implemented: onMessageReceived handles CHECKIN_REQUEST (3 action buttons), URGENT_ALERT (Call Now via DB lookup), LOCATION_ALERT (View Details). CheckInNotificationReceiver processes actions with location+battery, correct source mapping (ok=notification, need_help, call_me). Notification channels already in WellvoApplication.onCreate(). confirmDelivery() called on receive.

WellvoNavHost accepts notificationContext, routes CHECKIN_REQUEST->ReceiverHome, URGENT_ALERT/LOCATION_ALERT->OwnerTabs via LaunchedEffect. Content intents on all notification types launch MainActivity with extras. Badge cleared on handling.

Shows animated orange card with 'Enable' button linking to Settings.ACTION_APP_NOTIFICATION_SETTINGS. Displayed on DashboardScreen for owners. Auto-hides when permission granted.

Acknowledges purchases, verifies server-side via subscription-webhook. restorePurchases queries active subs. hasAccess/currentTier check entitlements. Handles UserCancelled, AlreadyOwned, NetworkError.

userStories.title: Implement location services for check-ins

acceptanceCriteria: LocationService @Singleton using FusedLocationProviderClient (Google Play Services)

Permission status checking: isLocationGranted(), isBackgroundLocationGranted()
Handle permission denied gracefully (check-in proceeds without location)
LocationService exposed via Hilt for injection into CheckInService and ReceiverViewModel
userStories.notes: LocationService @Singleton with FusedLocationProviderClient, 10s timeout, PRIORITY_BALANCED_POWER_ACCURACY (upgraded to HIGH_ACCURACY if fine permission granted). Permission helpers: isLocationGranted(), isBackgroundLocationGranted(), FOREGROUND_PERMISSIONS array, BACKGROUND_PERMISSION constant. getCurrentLocation() returns WellvoLocation or null. Graceful fallback to getLastKnownLocation() on timeout/error. Hilt-injectable via @Inject constructor.

userStories.title: Implement background location monitoring

Worker has network constraint (requires connectivity)
Monitoring started when receiver signs in with background location permission
Monitoring stopped on sign out
userStories.notes: Implemented. LocationReportWorker @HiltWorker with CoroutineWorker. 15-min periodic WorkManager schedule. Checks hasBackgroundLocationPermission before reporting. Gets current location + reports via ApiService.reportLocation. schedule/cancel static methods.

userStories.title: Implement heartbeat service with WorkManager

acceptanceCriteria: HeartbeatWorker extends CoroutineWorker: sends heartbeat to edge function with battery level and app version
Periodic work request: every 15 minutes (WorkManager minimum is 15 min)
Payload: batteryLevel (Float), appVersion (String from BuildConfig.VERSION_NAME)
HeartbeatService @Singleton: start() enqueues periodic work, stop() cancels it
Network constraint: only runs when connected
Battery level from BatteryManager sticky intent
Started on successful auth, stopped on sign out
Worker tagged 'heartbeat' for cancellation
userStories.notes: Implemented. HeartbeatWorker @HiltWorker with CoroutineWorker. 15-min periodic WorkManager schedule. Sends batteryLevel + BuildConfig.VERSION_NAME via ApiService.heartbeat. schedule/cancel static methods. Retries on failure.

userStories.title: Add request body size limits to edge functions server
